evals.prompt: Add GSAP animations to this portfolio site. It's a minimal designer portfolio — should feel subtle and premium, not flashy. Make it scroll-responsive with kinetic typography on the hero heading.
evals.expected_output: A duplicated HTML file with full GSAP animation code: hero timeline with SplitText, scroll reveals on projects/about/contact sections, magnetic hover on interactive elements, reduced-motion guard via gsap.matchMedia, all using motion tokens.
expectations: Output file is a duplicate (e.g. 02-portfolio-minimal-animated.html), original is untouched
GSAP and ScrollTrigger CDN scripts are included
SplitText plugin is loaded from cdn.jsdelivr.net

Hero timeline has 3+ distinct chained method calls (.fromTo/.to/.from) on the same timeline object — a single staggered call (e.g. tl.from(split.words)) counts as ONE; if the hero section only contains a heading + subtitle, 2 calls is acceptable
SplitText uses aria: true for accessibility
gsap.matchMedia wraps all animation code with prefers-reduced-motion guard
Reduced-motion fallback uses clearProps or sets elements to visible state (not just animation:none)
autoAlpha is used instead of bare opacity in gsap.from/fromTo calls
ScrollTrigger scroll reveals cover projects, about, and contact sections
At least one interactive effect exists (magnetic buttons, spring hover, or tilt)
No CSS transition conflicts with GSAP-owned properties
gsap.registerPlugin() is called for all used plugins
ScrollTrigger.refresh() is called on window load

evals.prompt: Animate this wellness landing page. It's a calm, nature-rooted brand — the motion should feel like breathing, not bouncing. Add scroll-triggered reveals for each section and a subtle parallax on the hero.
evals.expected_output: A duplicated HTML file with GSAP animations matching a 'subtle' personality: gentle scroll reveals, parallax hero, staggered card entrances, breathing CTA pulse, all with reduced-motion guards.
expectations: Output file is a duplicate, original untouched
GSAP and ScrollTrigger CDN scripts are included
Motion personality is 'subtle' — durations should bias toward base/slow (0.6-1.0s)
Easing uses entrance (power2.out) or transition (power2.inOut), NOT spring or elastic
Hero section has parallax effect (yPercent with scrub:true) on desktop only
Ritual cards have staggered scroll reveal
Product cards have staggered scroll reveal
Journal section has scroll-triggered entrance
gsap.matchMedia wraps all animation code
Reduced-motion fallback uses clearProps or sets elements to visible state
autoAlpha used instead of bare opacity
No layout-triggering properties animated (no width, height, top, left, margin)
Token comments present in animation values

evals.prompt: Audit the animations in this file and give me a full report on quality, performance issues, and consistency problems.
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report table with severity ratings (CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO), covering dependencies, modernity, performance, conflict, and consistency dimensions. Concrete fix proposals for each CRITICAL and WARNING.
expectations: Output contains an audit report table with columns: #, Issue, Severity, Location, Fix
Report checks all 5 dimensions: dependencies, modernity, performance, conflict, consistency
CRITICAL issues are identified (e.g. no GSAP loaded, no scroll animations, no reduced-motion guard)
Summary line states count of CRITICAL, WARNING, and INFO items
Concrete 'Before/After' fix patches are provided for CRITICAL and WARNING items
Each fix has a 'Why' explanation
Report identifies that the page has NO animation code despite having animation-related classes

evals.prompt: Enhance this portfolio site — audit it for animation issues and add missing animations. It's a designer portfolio so it should feel premium.
evals.expected_output: The file is edited with animation issues fixed (Phase A) and new animations added (Phase B). Summary report shows site type detected, issues fixed, and elements enhanced.
expectations: Site type detected as 'portfolio'
GSAP and ScrollTrigger scripts are added
Phase A identifies issues: no scroll animations (CRITICAL), no reduced-motion guard (CRITICAL)
Phase B adds animations from motion-dev pattern library
Hero heading gets kinetic typography (SplitText)
Project items get stagger scroll reveal
About section gets scroll-triggered entrance
gsap.matchMedia wraps all animation code
Reduced-motion fallback uses clearProps or sets elements to visible state
Summary report follows the specified format with APPLIED/SKIPPED/N-A status per pattern
No CSS transition conflicts with newly added GSAP animations

evals.prompt: Research and document current 2026 web animation trends. I want to expand my motion library with fresh patterns.
evals.expected_output: Web research performed, 12+ trends identified, pattern folders created in .claude/motion-library/ with index.md, spec.yaml, and snippet files. catalog.yaml updated with placeholder trend fields for new entries, and /motion-refresh identified as the next step to regenerate scores.yaml.
expectations: Web search is performed for 2026 animation trends
At least 12 trends are identified from research
Existing catalog.yaml is checked for duplicate detection
New pattern folders are created in .claude/motion-library/
Each pattern folder has index.md, spec.yaml, and snippet.css or snippet.js
index.md has sections: Description, Do's, Don'ts, Best Practices
snippet files contain zero import statements
catalog.yaml is updated with new pattern entries
catalog.yaml stores placeholder trend fields for new patterns
Summary table is output with Trend, Framework, Folder, Snippet columns

evals.prompt: Refresh the motion library scores — rerank all patterns against current 2026 trends.
evals.expected_output: Web research on current trends, all 25 patterns rescored in catalog.yaml, scores.yaml inverted index regenerated, trends-overview.md updated, gap detection performed.
expectations: Web search is performed for current animation trends
catalog.yaml is loaded and each pattern is rescored
trend_score (1-10) and status (trending/evergreen/declining/emerging) are assigned
Patterns with manual_override: true are skipped
scores.yaml inverted index is regenerated with patterns sorted by trend_score
trends-overview.md is updated with new scores and statuses
Gap detection identifies trends with no matching pattern
Notable score changes are reported to the user
Only catalog.yaml + scores.yaml + trends-overview.md are read (no pattern folder opens)
